_lesson_prefixes_note: DEPRECATED since 0.10.0 and no longer decides what counts as a lesson: a lesson is any *.md in the root of memory_dir except core_file, catalog_file and private _* files, and the lesson TYPE is read from the frontmatter `type` field. Lesson files are written by Claude Code's auto-memory, not by the engine, so requiring a filename prefix meant requiring something the engine does not control. Kept for backward compatibility in narrow roles: fallback for size_warn_prefixes and precedent_files, link matching in precedent_index. Mirrors Claude Code's official type vocabulary.
_precedent_files_note: Explicit precedent-accumulator files for auto-archiving (a WRITE path: it cuts cards out of the file). null → historical behaviour: the FIRST element of lesson_prefixes. Set it explicitly if your prefix order differs — the positional contract was never documented and silently targeted the wrong file.
topic_order: Hot core (mirrored in the core file)
no_topic_title: ⚠ No topic (add `topic:` to the frontmatter to file it here)
catalog_auto_start: <!-- AUTO-INDEX:START — managed by catalog_generate; edits between markers are overwritten -->
_task_close_pattern_note: Mirrors the library default. Covers all NINE GitHub closing keywords (close/closes/closed, fix/fixes/fixed, resolve/resolves/resolved) in BOTH documented spellings — `Closes #10` and `Closes: #10`. Two branches on purpose: after a colon only a NUMERIC id is accepted, because a slug id is this engine's own convention (GitHub ignores non-numeric ids), while `word: #slug` at the start of a subject line is Conventional Commits — measured on a live 2258-commit history, the permissive form produced 8 false closures and this one produced 0. The left boundary is a negative lookbehind, not \b, so that `auto-closes #10` is not misread as closing #10. Localise it for your team's wording, but keep both spellings of the nine English forms.
_task_close_command_watch_note: Second SOURCE of the closing signal, added in 0.13.0: the `gh issue close` command itself, watched via PostToolUse on Bash. The pattern above only ever sees commit text, and a command that creates no commit is invisible to it — after a tracker moves to GitHub Issues that gap swallows nearly every lesson. Widening the PATTERN cannot help here; the pattern is correct, the source was too narrow. Turn this off only if closing a task and recording its lesson deliberately happen in different sessions.

messages.unit.chars: (example) override any operator string here; English defaults live in claude_memory/messages.py
